brief: GAP/WHY: Catheter ablation curative for paroxysmal SVT, but diagnosing atrial tachycardia (AT) during EP study is often difficult; especially hard when SVT terminates upon pacing. Need a reliable maneuver to distinguish AT from other SVT mechanisms. PURPOSE: Develop and validate a novel pacing maneuver for AT diagnosis. DESIGN/SETTING: Prospective electrophysiology study. POPULATION/N: 147 SVTs studied — 28 ATs, 87 AVNRTs (AV nodal reentrant tachycardia), 32 ORTs (orthodromic reciprocating tachycardia). Non-AT group = AVNRT + ORT = 119. INTERVENTION/MANEUVER: Single atrial extrastimulation delivered from proximal coronary sinus during tachycardia; coupling interval started 20 ms shorter than tachycardia cycle length, progressively decreased until His bundle (HB) first reset, then further until SVT terminated. Assessed two things: (a) response of SVT at first HB resetting, (b) termination pattern. KEY FINDINGS at first HB reset: 27/28 ATs = tachycardia UNAFFECTED when HB reset. In non-AT (AVNRT/ORT): tachycardia simultaneously reset at HB resetting OR terminated with atrio-Hisian (A-H) block. TERMINATION PATTERN (further shortening when tachycardia persisted): all 33 SVTs terminating with atrio-Hisian block were non-AT; among those terminating with Hisian-atrial (H-A) block = 5 ATs + 7 non-ATs. DIAGNOSTIC PERFORMANCE — pattern of tachycardia unaffected at HB resetting for AT diagnosis: sensitivity 96%, specificity 100%, PPV 100%, NPV 99%. Pattern of termination with atrio-Hisian block for non-AT diagnosis: sensitivity 92%, specificity 100%, PPV 100%, NPV 42%. TAKEAWAY: Single atrial extrastimulation from proximal coronary sinus during tachycardia is a useful, effective maneuver for diagnosing AT; AT is characterized by tachycardia remaining unaffected during His bundle resetting.
